agent_approach_summary: The agent identified that the issue involved MARC 880 alternate-script handling and began refactoring the MARC abstractions, adding a base field interface, adding 880 to requested fields, and attempting linkage/publisher/subject changes. However, it never completed the parser changes needed across title/author/TOC/normalization paths and did not run the new fail-to-pass tests.
difficulty_evidence: The new tests are behavioral and aligned with the problem statement: they check extraction of linked and unlinked 880 metadata, de-duplication of series, and normalization of publisher abbreviations. The agent's edits were incomplete and likely incorrect for the required behavior: it only partially handled publishers, introduced an ad hoc get_linkage API, did not implement the linked-880 title/other_titles logic, did not handle alternate names or table of contents extraction, and never validated against the failing tests before timing out.
reasoning: This looks clean rather than contaminated. The problem statement explicitly says 880 fields should be parsed in both linked and unlinked scenarios and mentions normalization issues such as duplicate series and abbreviation formatting. The F2P tests check exactly those kinds of observable outputs on MARC fixtures: alternate-script title/publisher/place extraction, related metadata coming from linked 880s, series de-duplication, and corrected publisher formatting. They do not lock the solution to gold-patch internals such as helper names, class structure, or exact implementation strategy. The gold patch includes substantial refactoring (new abstract base class, read_fields/get_control/get_linkage changes), but that appears to be overpatch/engineering structure, not something the tests require. In the trajectory, the agent understood the bug category but produced only partial, likely insufficient changes and then exited without submitting a working patch, so the failure is best explained by incomplete implementation/time rather than hidden or unfair test requirements.
